fuzz-jam-june.md.answer: Jochen Hilgers provides an in-depth look at the history, present, and future of fuzzing in his video presentation regarding modern fuzzing, which includes live fuzzer use kata.

when-we-was-chip-fab-focused.md.question: What technologies are included in the poll assessing reader perspectives on future impact?
when-we-was-chip-fab-focused.md.answer: The poll assesses artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), CRISPR, pentatricopeptide repeats (PPR), fifth generation cellular networking (5G), and industrial Internet of Things (IIoT).

when-we-was-chip-fab-focused.md.question: What factors influence where semiconductor fabrication facility investments are likely to land?
when-we-was-chip-fab-focused.md.answer: Investments are likely to benefit localities that offer the right mix of tax incentives, seismic stability, energy, and water rights.

when-we-was-chip-fab-focused.md.question: What is the primary vision of the future discussed regarding computing?
when-we-was-chip-fab-focused.md.answer: The vision is to create a 'whim based infrastructure' where computation is ubiquitous and manual interactions are replaced with informed actions that have imperceptible latencies, effectively connecting to human intuition and whims.

proxy-tear-us-apart.md.question: Why did the author decide to move their newsletter from Substack to Buttondown?
proxy-tear-us-apart.md.answer: The author moved to Buttondown because they wanted access to a public API and more design flexibility, preferring a platform that caters to their needs as an aspiring developer and writer rather than a 'walled garden' service.

proxy-tear-us-apart.md.question: What is the author's opinion on the future of VPNs in light of new offerings from cloud providers?
proxy-tear-us-apart.md.answer: The author does not believe the VPN market will become irrelevant; however, they believe that as major cloud providers move toward Beyondcorp-esque offerings, VPNs will likely be pushed into more niche use cases.

proxy-tear-us-apart.md.question: What development tools does the author mention that will support the transition to zero trust designs?
proxy-tear-us-apart.md.answer: The author notes that infrastructure as code (IaC) tools such as Terraform, Ansible, and Pulumi will enable incremental approaches to zero trust design.

find-your-rod-and-reel.md.question: What is the primary function of a pre-sales engineer in an enterprise sales context?
find-your-rod-and-reel.md.answer: The pre-sales engineer provides the necessary subject matter expertise to support technology pre-sales when a company is working to acquire a new customer, typically acting as a tandem partner to an account executive.

find-your-rod-and-reel.md.question: What does the backronym ROD stand for in the context of AE and SE pairings?
find-your-rod-and-reel.md.answer: ROD stands for Relevant, Organized, and Direct.

find-your-rod-and-reel.md.question: What does the backronym REEL stand for in the context of AE and SE pairings?
find-your-rod-and-reel.md.answer: REEL stands for Responsive, Effective, Efficient, and Likable.

build-a-cloud-platform-foundation.md.question: How does the author define developer experience (DevX/DevEx)?
build-a-cloud-platform-foundation.md.answer: The author defines developer experience as the empathy that an organization expresses for their developers to enable and empower them.

build-a-cloud-platform-foundation.md.question: What is the definition of platform engineering provided in the post?
build-a-cloud-platform-foundation.md.answer: Platform engineering is defined as the discipline of designing and building toolchains and workflows that enable self-service capabilities for software engineering organizations in the cloud-native era.

build-a-cloud-platform-foundation.md.question: What are the four key questions digital organizations should answer regarding their developer strategy?
build-a-cloud-platform-foundation.md.answer: The four key questions are: Do you enable a culture that attracts developers? Do you have a clear platform engineering strategy? Do you promote using a DevX/DevEx minded cloud platform foundation? Do you further enrich the foundation with developer goal landing zones?

back-to-my-consulting-roots.md.question: What is the author's professional background?
back-to-my-consulting-roots.md.answer: The author has served as a CTO since 2002 with NeoNova and Digitel.

back-to-my-consulting-roots.md.question: Where is the author located?
back-to-my-consulting-roots.md.answer: The author has relocated to Research Triangle Park, NC, and has also established a second office in Missoula, MT.

back-to-my-consulting-roots.md.question: Where can clients find the author's consulting services?
back-to-my-consulting-roots.md.answer: Clients can find the author and updated content at cuthrell.com.

shock-the-chaos-monkey.md.question: What is the core focus of chaos engineering for platform engineering teams?
shock-the-chaos-monkey.md.answer: Chaos engineering goes beyond Murphy's Law by actively thinking and acting upon events that might or will go wrong to ensure system resilience.

shock-the-chaos-monkey.md.question: What question does the author suggest asking to frame a discussion about chaos engineering?
shock-the-chaos-monkey.md.answer: The author suggests asking how confident a team is that the remaining software development team could handle everything that occurs if the top SRE or platform team members were to take an extended vacation.

shock-the-chaos-monkey.md.question: Has the topic of chaos engineering been covered by this blog previously?
shock-the-chaos-monkey.md.answer: Yes, chaos engineering was previously covered in 2021 in Fudge Sunday #113 as part of a five-part series on 'Cloud in Public'.

insert-comment.md.question: Why did the author decide to turn off comments on their blog?
insert-comment.md.answer: After testing various comment systems and reviewing the total number of comments received, the author concluded that maintaining them was unnecessary and silly.

insert-comment.md.question: Has the author removed social sharing options from their blog?
insert-comment.md.answer: Yes, the author has removed sharing options because they no longer care about the social impact, noting that they can track engagement through their own web server logs.

insert-comment.md.question: Is the author planning to switch to a Tumblr-style platform for their blog?
insert-comment.md.answer: The author is unsure, stating that while they are curious about trying new platforms, they are currently considering options like PaaS to avoid tasks such as running OS updates.

mind-mapping.md.question: What is the primary function of a mind map as described in the post?
mind-mapping.md.answer: A mind map is a visual tool that typically begins with a specific subject or primary topic, which then branches out into subtopics and subtopics of subtopics to organize information.

mind-mapping.md.question: Which software does the author use for their personal knowledge management and Zettelkasten?
mind-mapping.md.answer: The author uses Obsidian to manage their knowledge base and has been expanding their vaults since 2021.

mind-mapping.md.question: What is the stated goal of A.I. agents according to the blog post?
mind-mapping.md.answer: The goal of A.I. agents is to harness technologies like personal knowledge graphs, vector databases, synthetic data, and GenAI to provide humans and other A.I. agents with API-accessible applications that offer high-utility value.

back-in-circulation.md.question: Which upcoming events is the author planning to attend?
back-in-circulation.md.answer: The author is planning to attend Dell Technologies World in Las Vegas later this month and Qlik Connect in Orlando next month, where they will serve as a Tech Field Day delegate.

back-in-circulation.md.question: Which mobile app does the author recommend for tracking flights?
back-in-circulation.md.answer: The author recommends the app Flighty, noting that it provides better flight status, ETA, and visualizations than the native airline apps.

back-in-circulation.md.question: What is the author's new initiative regarding postcards?
back-in-circulation.md.answer: The author is inviting readers to send snail mail postcards. They plan to scan the picture side of the postcards received and feature them in a gallery on the Fudge Sunday newsletter.

happy-when-it-toolchains.md.question: What is a software toolchain?
happy-when-it-toolchains.md.answer: A software toolchain is defined as a set of tools used during the activities associated with the creation and modification of various programs throughout their lifespan.

happy-when-it-toolchains.md.question: Are toolchains uniform across different companies?
happy-when-it-toolchains.md.answer: No, a toolchain is not one fixed thing. It is a composable set of tools that depends on the specific needs of the organization, such as the size of the developer team, the diversity of the business portfolio, and the regulatory environment.

happy-when-it-toolchains.md.question: What is the primary role of Cloud Service Providers (CSPs) regarding toolchains?
happy-when-it-toolchains.md.answer: CSPs often promote Cloud Adoption Frameworks (CAF) that reference specific tools, though these recommendations may carry a bias towards the CSP's own proprietary tools.

read-the-fabulous-manual.md.question: What is the alternative meaning the author proposes for the acronym RTFM?
read-the-fabulous-manual.md.answer: The author prefers to interpret RTFM as 'Read The Fabulous Manual' instead of the commonly used vulgar variation.

read-the-fabulous-manual.md.question: How does the blog post use the term 'TPS' in the context of organizations?
read-the-fabulous-manual.md.answer: In organizations that lack a strong documentarian population, the author notes that 'TPS' acts as an acronym or backronym for 'This Place Struggles,' 'This Place Sucks,' or 'Tragically Preventable Scenario/Situation'.

read-the-fabulous-manual.md.question: What is the significance of DocBook and DITA in the history of documentation?
read-the-fabulous-manual.md.answer: DocBook was introduced in 1991 as a portable self-contained documentation schema project, while DITA (Darwin Information Typing Architecture) emerged a few years later as a standard for information typing and now has a community supported by the DITA Open Toolkit.

looking-for-looking-for.md.question: What does the author suggest is the best way to improve your odds of finding a job compared to just applying through applicant tracking systems?
looking-for-looking-for.md.answer: The author suggests that instead of relying on applicant tracking systems, which have very low success rates, you should talk to people and leverage your network to connect with the right individuals.

looking-for-looking-for.md.question: Why does the author discourage relying solely on applicant tracking systems when job hunting?
looking-for-looking-for.md.answer: The author explains that applying through these systems is similar to buying a lottery ticket, noting that in the realm of knowledge work, the odds of success are typically between 1 in 1,000 or 1 in 10,000.

looking-for-looking-for.md.question: How can LinkedIn search parameters be used to find recent job openings within one's network?
looking-for-looking-for.md.answer: The author recommends searching for keywords like 'hiring' or 'looking' and filtering the results to show only posts from your 1st-degree connections that were posted within the last 24 hours.

idp-wasnt-built-in-a-day.md.question: What is an Internal Developer Platform (IDP) in the context of platform engineering?
idp-wasnt-built-in-a-day.md.answer: An Internal Developer Platform (IDP) is defined as a uniquely custom stack or toolchain, which is supported by a platform team.

idp-wasnt-built-in-a-day.md.question: What similarity does the author identify between platform engineering and RevOps/DataOps teams?
idp-wasnt-built-in-a-day.md.answer: The author notes that both platform engineering teams and RevOps/DataOps teams must maintain and streamline pipelines—CI/CD pipelines for platform engineering and data pipelines for GTM teams—to increase productivity and improve operations.

idp-wasnt-built-in-a-day.md.question: What risk does the author highlight regarding manually intensive pipelines in GTM teams?
idp-wasnt-built-in-a-day.md.answer: The author points out that relying on manually intensive pipelines leads to 'single-point-of-vacation-failure' engineers. When key team members depart, these precarious, undocumented heroic pipelines fail, causing the organization to suffer from revenue losses and wasted opportunity costs.

little-green-tags.md.question: What is the focus of the blog post regarding FinOps?
little-green-tags.md.answer: The blog post explores the past, present, and future of FinOps strategies specifically related to A.I. workloads.

little-green-tags.md.question: Why might businesses need to reconsider their current cloud tagging and metadata strategies?
little-green-tags.md.answer: Because businesses are increasingly being asked to integrate A.I. into public clouds, leading to questions about whether traditional cloud operating models and adoption frameworks are sufficient for these new workloads.

little-green-tags.md.question: What is the alternative to using hyperscale public cloud providers for A.I. workloads mentioned in the post?
little-green-tags.md.answer: The post mentions the potential for avoiding hyperscale public cloud providers entirely by embracing cloud repatriation.

who-said-the-ai-ml-was-fair.md.question: What does the acronym FAIR stand for in the context of research data principles?
who-said-the-ai-ml-was-fair.md.answer: FAIR stands for Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable principles.

who-said-the-ai-ml-was-fair.md.question: Are FAIR principles considered an established, long-standing standard in IT?
who-said-the-ai-ml-was-fair.md.answer: No, FAIR principles are less than a decade old, which is considered young in IT terms.

who-said-the-ai-ml-was-fair.md.question: What is the concern mentioned regarding the future of data and market incentives?
who-said-the-ai-ml-was-fair.md.answer: The post expresses concern that perverse market incentives might drive data to become more closed, proprietary, and paywalled, focusing on short-term extraction rather than long-term enlightenment and human values.

connected.md.question: What is the primary niche the author is considering for their next newsletter?
connected.md.answer: The author is considering focusing on the domain where telecom, cloud, and A.I. are meeting, colliding, and converging, specifically taking a conceptual approach to ICT generations.

connected.md.question: Has the author considered becoming an industry analyst?
connected.md.answer: Yes, the author has discussed this with industry peers and noted that while it is flattering to be viewed as an analyst, they are currently focused on their fractional executive journey and exploring the conceptual aspects of ICT.

connected.md.question: What are the potential names the author is considering for their next project?
connected.md.answer: The author is currently leaning towards naming the project either 'Fudge Factor' or 'Fudge Forward'.

busy-child.md.question: What are some examples of youth learning communities mentioned in the blog post?
busy-child.md.answer: The blog post highlights Scratch, DIY, Makers Empire, and Code.org as examples of communities for young learners.

busy-child.md.question: What kind of statistics does the Scratch community have?
busy-child.md.answer: Scratch grows by over 1 million accounts each month, is used in every country on the planet, and is available in more than 70 languages.

busy-child.md.question: What is the mission of Code.org?
busy-child.md.answer: Code.org seeks to reach students in underserved populations and expose them to computer science, particularly focusing on groups that are historically underrepresented in STEM fields.

reflections-on-a-dot-com-legacy.md.question: What was the author's first experience with a corporate merger?
reflections-on-a-dot-com-legacy.md.answer: The author's first merger occurred when Bay Networks was acquired by Nortel while the author was working in a small Nortel group.

reflections-on-a-dot-com-legacy.md.question: Which company acquired iXL in 2001?
reflections-on-a-dot-com-legacy.md.answer: iXL was acquired by their competitor, Scient, following the bursting of the dot-com bubble.

reflections-on-a-dot-com-legacy.md.question: How did the companies the author worked for eventually evolve over two decades?
reflections-on-a-dot-com-legacy.md.answer: The companies the author worked for were part of a long series of nested mergers and roll-ups that eventually led to them becoming part of Publicis, one of the largest communications companies in the world.

fediverse-moving-over-the-face-of-the-wa.question: Why is the author moving their newsletter from Substack to Buttondown?
fediverse-moving-over-the-face-of-the-wa.answer: The author decided to switch to Buttondown because Substack's default sharing links were not using their custom domain, leading them to reconsider the platform and seek a more simplified, self-managed solution.

fediverse-moving-over-the-face-of-the-wa.question: What steps were involved in migrating the newsletter to Buttondown?
fediverse-moving-over-the-face-of-the-wa.answer: The migration process included exporting posts and subscriber data from Substack, importing that data into Buttondown, purchasing a Buttondown Standard subscription, updating DNS settings for their custom domain, and customizing the newsletter settings.

fediverse-moving-over-the-face-of-the-wa.question: How did the author transition from Twitter to Mastodon?
fediverse-moving-over-the-face-of-the-wa.answer: The author set up a single-user Mastodon instance on their own domain using a Linode VPS. They used Fedifinder to export their Twitter followers and lists, imported that data into Mastodon, and updated their profile to reflect their new Mastodon handle.

here-it-comes.md.question: How early should I arrive at the airport for my flight?
here-it-comes.md.answer: You should arrive at the airport a minimum of two hours before departure for domestic flights and a minimum of two and a half hours for international flights.

here-it-comes.md.question: What forms of identification are considered acceptable for passengers?
here-it-comes.md.answer: Acceptable forms of identification include a valid photo driver’s license, an active passport, or employee identification from a county, state, or federal agency.

here-it-comes.md.question: Are non-ticketed individuals allowed to go to the gate area?
here-it-comes.md.answer: No, only individuals with valid travel documentation, such as an electronic ticket confirmation, itinerary, boarding pass, or paper ticket, are permitted beyond security checkpoints; those without tickets cannot enter gate areas to greet or see off passengers.

how-soon-is-next-year.md.question: What are the two specific predictions the author highlights for 2023?
how-soon-is-next-year.md.answer: The two predictions highlighted for 2023 are Assumed Accessible Observability and the growth of Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) cultures.

how-soon-is-next-year.md.question: How does the author define the difference between Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) and Platform Engineering (PE) in terms of empathy?
how-soon-is-next-year.md.answer: The author suggests that if the SRE ethos is grounded in empathy for the end user of a digital service, then the Platform Engineering ethos is grounded in empathy for the developers of a digital service.

how-soon-is-next-year.md.question: Why does the author believe that 2023 will see an evolution in data-driven journalism?
how-soon-is-next-year.md.answer: The author believes that as machine learning and artificial intelligence proliferate, being able to 'watch the watchers' of these new machines through accessible debug logs will become an evolved form of data-driven journalism.

road-warrior-redux.md.question: What items does the author currently carry in their bag as of 2019?
road-warrior-redux.md.answer: As of 2019, the author carries two laptops (one for work and one for personal use), a high-wattage power adapter, two mobile phones on different carriers, a full-size charging brick with cables, three lounge memberships (Delta, American Airlines, and AMEX Centurion), and a slim or padded backpack.

road-warrior-redux.md.question: What advice does the author offer regarding airport lounge selection?
road-warrior-redux.md.answer: The author suggests that travelers should choose a lounge based on personal preference for amenities, such as electrical outlet availability, Wi-Fi coverage, and dietary options, rather than solely choosing based on the proximity to their departure gate.

road-warrior-redux.md.question: Why does the author recommend checking one's large bag instead of carrying it on?
road-warrior-redux.md.answer: The author recommends checking large bags to experience the freedom of walking through the airport terminal with only a backpack, noting that while there is a small risk of issues with checked luggage, clothes and shoes can be replaced.

deploy-everything-you-touch.md.question: How many deployments per week did Facebook report in 2016?
deploy-everything-you-touch.md.answer: In 2016, research papers indicated that Facebook was achieving approximately 5,000 deployments per week.

deploy-everything-you-touch.md.question: What is the name of the internal tool used by Facebook for continuous deployment?
deploy-everything-you-touch.md.answer: Facebook's internal tool for continuous deployment is called Conveyor.

deploy-everything-you-touch.md.question: Is continuous deployment only suitable for large B2C companies like Meta?
deploy-everything-you-touch.md.answer: No, while the numbers at large B2C companies can be dizzying, continuous deployment is becoming increasingly relevant across every industry as more companies embrace digital transformation.

have-your-ai-call-my-ai.md.question: What tools does the author currently use to help with meeting scheduling?
have-your-ai-call-my-ai.md.answer: The author uses several alternatives, including World Clock Meeting Planner, SunCalc, World Time Buddy, Time Zones, and When2meet.

have-your-ai-call-my-ai.md.question: What is the author's general stance on using Artificial Intelligence for scheduling tasks?
have-your-ai-call-my-ai.md.answer: The author is skeptical about the role of AI in meeting scheduling, noting that their past experiences with AI-ish tools and calendar integration startups have yielded mixed results.

have-your-ai-call-my-ai.md.question: What happened to the company x.ai according to the blog post updates?
have-your-ai-call-my-ai.md.answer: According to an update from April 14, 2023, x.ai was acquired by Bizzabo in 2021, and the domain name was repurposed, which the author notes leads to link rot.

uh-zoom-zpr.md.question: What is the primary goal of Zero-Trust Packet Routing (ZPR)?
uh-zoom-zpr.md.answer: The goal of ZPR is to create an identity-aware network security layer, known as a ZPRnet, which allows organizations to enforce uniform security policies across all their systems and users regardless of whether they are on-premises, in clouds, or using remote connections.

uh-zoom-zpr.md.question: How are security policies determined within a ZPRnet?
uh-zoom-zpr.md.answer: Security policies in ZPR are based on the authenticated identity and attributes of both the communicators and the communicated data.

uh-zoom-zpr.md.question: Where can one find more information about Zero-Trust Packet Routing?
uh-zoom-zpr.md.answer: Additional information can be found at the official website zpr.org, the ZPR publication page on PubPub, or by reviewing the published paper via its Digital Object Identifier (DOI): 10.21428/60abf910.5e8e3001.

saved-by-env0.md.question: What is the primary function of the company env0?
saved-by-env0.md.answer: env0 is a company that helps to simplify and streamline the adoption of Infrastructure as Code (IaC) associated with public cloud operating models.

saved-by-env0.md.question: What specific IaC features does env0 offer that are mentioned in the post?
saved-by-env0.md.answer: env0 offers examples of ChatOps or GitOps, such as planning and applying from PR comments, as well as cost monitoring features for Infrastructure as Code.

saved-by-env0.md.question: How does the author characterize the maturity of the public cloud?
saved-by-env0.md.answer: The author characterizes the public cloud as being like a teenager, noting that while it is old enough to legally drive in the United States, it is not yet old enough to lease a car.

cat-diapers.md.question: How should pet fecal matter be disposed of in Raleigh?
cat-diapers.md.answer: According to the city's waste department, fecal matter should be removed from litter or diapers and flushed down the toilet so it can be properly treated by the sewage plant.

cat-diapers.md.question: Does the City of Raleigh allow pet waste or loose cat litter to be placed in the regular garbage cart?
cat-diapers.md.answer: No, the city advises against putting loose cat litter or animal waste in the garbage, noting that disposable diapers and animal litter should be free of fecal matter and enclosed in a plastic bag before being placed in a garbage cart.

cat-diapers.md.question: What is the purpose of the LitterLocker product mentioned in the post?
cat-diapers.md.answer: The LitterLocker is marketed as a product that allows pet owners to scoop and sift waste from a litter box and store it in a container for disposal.

jumping-someone-elses-trained-model.md.question: Who sees GenAI benefits outside of technologists?
jumping-someone-elses-trained-model.md.answer: The author notes that individuals outside of the technology field, including themselves, are beginning to see and experience the benefits of GenAI.

jumping-someone-elses-trained-model.md.question: What practical GenAI benefit can be experienced and grasped by business leaders?
jumping-someone-elses-trained-model.md.answer: A practical benefit for business leaders is the ability to use 'Generate' tools to create clear, crisp, and concise summaries of content, such as newsletters, as demonstrated by the author's own use of such technology.

jumping-someone-elses-trained-model.md.question: When will everyone begin to benefit from GenAI?
jumping-someone-elses-trained-model.md.answer: Expectations are that as access to the technology becomes democratized, commodified, and subsidized, the price per month will drop, allowing for wider adoption and benefits for everyone.

reading-fudge-sunday-test-2.md.question: What equipment is used to record the episode?
reading-fudge-sunday-test-2.md.answer: The episode is recorded using a Shure SM57 microphone and a PreSonus Studio 24c interface.

reading-fudge-sunday-test-2.md.question: What software is used for recording and editing the audio?
reading-fudge-sunday-test-2.md.answer: GarageBand is used to record and edit the audio for the episode.

reading-fudge-sunday-test-2.md.question: Where is the final audio file uploaded?
reading-fudge-sunday-test-2.md.answer: The rendered audio file is uploaded to the podcast publishing page on Substack.

pandas-cerebras-vast-weka-fhe.md.question: What is the primary prediction regarding the cloud operating model (COM) by 2026?
pandas-cerebras-vast-weka-fhe.md.answer: Gartner predicts that within two years, three in four operations leaders will use cloud platform foundations as the underpinning for digital transformation.

pandas-cerebras-vast-weka-fhe.md.question: What two factors does the author identify as essential for organizations to thrive in the coming years?
pandas-cerebras-vast-weka-fhe.md.answer: The author states that organizations will need to see both interoperability and convergence to succeed.

pandas-cerebras-vast-weka-fhe.md.question: What does the author suggest will be required for organizations that currently follow a 'cloud only' strategy?
pandas-cerebras-vast-weka-fhe.md.answer: The author suggests that any current provider with a 'cloud only' strategy will need to adjust their roadmaps, M&A strategy, and corporate development to contemplate edge-to-core-to-cloud infrastructure.

arguably-illusory.md.question: What does the author suggest the acronym A.I. may represent in the current tech cycle?
arguably-illusory.md.answer: The author suggests that in the current cycle, the acronym A.I. may simply stand for 'Arguably Illusory'.

arguably-illusory.md.question: What historical service does the author compare modern A.I. to?
arguably-illusory.md.answer: The author compares modern A.I. to the Amazon Mechanical Turk, a service launched nearly 20 years ago that relies on humans to perform tasks that may be mistaken for automation.

arguably-illusory.md.question: What is Human-in-the-loop (HITL) machine learning according to the post?
arguably-illusory.md.answer: HITL is described as the human training of A.I., which the author notes will continue to be part of the story even when approaches may appear to be purely mechanical or automated.

multicloud-summer-reading.md.question: What is the primary drawback of a 'cloud-first' strategy limited to a single provider?
multicloud-summer-reading.md.answer: A single-provider cloud-first strategy often leads to vendor lock-in due to data gravity, which makes it difficult to use innovative services from competing cloud providers and subjects the organization to significant egress fees when moving data.

multicloud-summer-reading.md.question: How does a 'data-first' approach differ from a 'cloud-first' approach?
multicloud-summer-reading.md.answer: A data-first approach prioritizes making data accessible to multiple clouds, which avoids vendor lock-in and enables innovation by allowing organizations to utilize services from various providers like GCP, Azure, or AWS as needed.

multicloud-summer-reading.md.question: What is the benefit of using a single, central copy of data in a multi-cloud architecture?
multicloud-summer-reading.md.answer: A central copy of data allows it to be simultaneously adjacent to all public cloud providers. This enables companies to take immediate advantage of innovation cycles from any provider, reduces costs by avoiding duplicate data copies, and streamlines workflows by using a shared file system.

5-years-of-probing.md.question: What is a RIPE Atlas probe?
5-years-of-probing.md.answer: A RIPE Atlas probe is a specialized network device that continuously measures Internet performance and relays key statistical information to an open community of individuals and companies.

5-years-of-probing.md.question: How do you set up a RIPE Atlas probe?
5-years-of-probing.md.answer: Setting up a probe involves plugging an Ethernet cable from your home router into the provided nanopi box and supplying it with USB power.

5-years-of-probing.md.question: What are the benefits of earning RIPE Atlas credits?
5-years-of-probing.md.answer: By earning credits, you gain access to the RIPE Atlas community, which allows you to run User-Defined Measurements (UDMs), such as checking ping times or testing SSL for a website from various locations around the planet.

20-years-of-blogging.md.question: Why did the author decide to include the year in blog post titles?
20-years-of-blogging.md.answer: The author added the year to post titles to provide context for readers, as previous visitors often commented on older posts without realizing they were written in a different time period.

20-years-of-blogging.md.question: How does the author view the loss of old blog posts?
20-years-of-blogging.md.answer: The author views the loss of blog posts as being similar to a writer's collection of wadded-up paper, choosing to consider those losses as opportunities to write better posts in the future.

20-years-of-blogging.md.question: Why does the author currently avoid writing about their company on their personal blog?
20-years-of-blogging.md.answer: The author believes that a personal blog is the worst place to discuss a company because there is no upside and it presents mostly downsides.

finding-my-niche.md.question: Why did the author decide to analyze their previous blog posts using JupyterLab?
finding-my-niche.md.answer: The author received reader feedback suggesting the newsletter topics were too broad and that they should focus on a specific niche. The author used JupyterLab to analyze their existing markdown posts to identify recurring themes and patterns to help determine a suitable focus.

finding-my-niche.md.question: What specific libraries does the analysis script use to process the markdown files?
finding-my-niche.md.answer: The script utilizes several libraries, including glob, markdown, re, nltk, collections.Counter, bs4 (BeautifulSoup), pandas, requests, textblob, gensim, and spacy.

finding-my-niche.md.question: What common themes emerged from the author's analysis of their past content?
finding-my-niche.md.answer: The analysis revealed that the author's writing frequently covers topics related to telecom, cloud computing, and A.I., which led the author to consider a niche that focuses on the intersection of the edge and machine learning.

idp-clearly-now.md.question: How does the author define an Internal Developer Platform (IDP)?
idp-clearly-now.md.answer: The author defines an Internal Developer Platform (IDP) as the prevailing toolchain within an organization that develops software applications, representing an opinionated stack that is adopted and refashioned over time.

idp-clearly-now.md.question: Can members of the public contribute to the definition of an Internal Developer Platform?
idp-clearly-now.md.answer: Yes, if you have ideas for a better definition of IDP, you can submit a pull request to the Internal Developer Platform website's GitHub repository.

idp-clearly-now.md.question: Is an Identity Provider (IdP) the same thing as an Internal Developer Platform (IDP)?
idp-clearly-now.md.answer: No, the author notes that it is important to distinguish between the two, as they are different acronyms; additionally, an Internal Developer Platform likely relies upon an Identity Provider to function.

summary-solstice.md.question: What is happening to the Hot Fudge Daily weekly digest?
summary-solstice.md.answer: This week's edition will be the final weekly digest in the series. Moving forward, the content will be incorporated into the 'Spicy Edition Sunday' newsletter as free weekly content to streamline the author's workflow.

summary-solstice.md.question: Why is the author planning to add the Google Analytics 4 tag back to their website?
summary-solstice.md.answer: The author intends to use the statistics to measure how 'Fudge Sunday' and 'Hot Fudge Daily' contribute to 'Cuthrell Consulting,' as strong analytics are important for attracting potential newsletter sponsorships.

summary-solstice.md.question: How can readers participate in the new 'Fudge Sunday Reader Postcards' project?
summary-solstice.md.answer: Readers can send a postcard to the author; the author plans to scan the picture side of received postcards and feature them in a gallery, ensuring suitable redactions and anonymity are applied.

around-the-bend.md.question: What is the newsletter's current platform status?
around-the-bend.md.answer: The newsletter has successfully migrated from Revue (Twitter) to Substack.

around-the-bend.md.question: What happened to the iPod Touch product line in 2022?
around-the-bend.md.answer: Apple discontinued the iPod touch, ending the product line after selling remaining inventory 'while supplies last'.

around-the-bend.md.question: What challenges did the author face during the migration to Substack?
around-the-bend.md.answer: The author encountered issues such as 'null' import results that required manual deletion and re-importing, as well as malformed YouTube links that necessitate manual editing.

under-the-guise-of-simplicity.md.question: Why does the author suggest that people should use voice communication more often?
under-the-guise-of-simplicity.md.answer: The author suggests using voice more if individuals are spending too much time in email or experiencing frustration with instant messaging, as voice allows for immediate, symmetric communication and the benefits of proxemics.

under-the-guise-of-simplicity.md.question: What are the primary limitations of instant messaging as a communication tool?
under-the-guise-of-simplicity.md.answer: Instant messaging is at the mercy of presence, meaning the sender cannot control whether the recipient is reading or comprehending the message. Additionally, users can walk away from their devices, which can be problematic if the person is in a critical path for a remote team.

under-the-guise-of-simplicity.md.question: How can companies address the abuse of email features and flow?
under-the-guise-of-simplicity.md.answer: Companies can share email etiquette and guides for sending better emails with their team members, a process the author notes can ironically be accomplished via email.

a-new-map.md.question: Who is the speaker of the OSCON 2015 keynote talk titled 'Situation Normal, Everything Must Change'?
According to the blog post, why is it important to remember that a map is not a static concept?
a-new-map.md.answer: The post notes that when creating a map, there is a tendency to forget about position, movement, and time in transit, so it is necessary to constantly remind yourself and your peers that a map is not a static or exhaustive treatment of a concept.

a-new-map.md.question: What movie does the author reference to illustrate the point that sometimes one needs to get a new map?
a-new-map.md.answer: The author references the 1995 movie 'Tommy Boy'.

press-rewind.md.question: What is the focus of this week's Fudge Sunday issue?
press-rewind.md.answer: This week's issue focuses on recent updates in software supply chain security and the ability to effectively 'press rewind' when security issues occur.

press-rewind.md.question: How does the author characterize the flexibility of VoIP office phones?
press-rewind.md.answer: The author notes that while VoIP phones offer software-definable characteristics and flexibility based on embedded Linux, this same flexibility allows for unintended uses, such as running games like Doom on a desk phone.

press-rewind.md.question: Beyond tools, what does the author emphasize is necessary for software supply chain security?
press-rewind.md.answer: The author emphasizes that software supply chain security requires more than just tools scanning tools; it also requires the involvement of people, processes, and community knowledge sharing.

linkedin-lists-looking-back-and-life.md.question: What happens if a LinkedIn user makes enough contributions to collaborative articles?
linkedin-lists-looking-back-and-life.md.answer: If a user makes enough contributions, LinkedIn offers them a Community Top Voice badge.

linkedin-lists-looking-back-and-life.md.question: How does the author describe the 'shot and chaser' format used in the blog post?
linkedin-lists-looking-back-and-life.md.answer: The format is a callback to prior topics or links, which are then compared to a more recent or current link for the week.

linkedin-lists-looking-back-and-life.md.question: What is the author's current status on LinkedIn?
linkedin-lists-looking-back-and-life.md.answer: The author has a profile set to Creator mode with under 4,000 connections and over 4,200 followers, and they do not plan to quit the platform yet.

i-want-to-thank-you.md.question: How long has the author been writing on Substack?
i-want-to-thank-you.md.answer: The author has been writing on Substack for five months.

i-want-to-thank-you.md.question: Where do the majority of the author's new subscribers come from?
i-want-to-thank-you.md.answer: 60% of new subscribers come from Substack platform features.

i-want-to-thank-you.md.question: What is the author's stated goal for the content in their newsletter?
i-want-to-thank-you.md.answer: The author strives to bring education, utility, a sprinkle of humor, and humility to the newsletter.

please-please-otel-me-now.md.question: What is OpenTelemetry (Otel)?
please-please-otel-me-now.md.answer: OpenTelemetry, or Otel, is an open standard designed for generating, collecting, and exporting telemetry in a vendor-agnostic way.

please-please-otel-me-now.md.question: How was OpenTelemetry formed?
please-please-otel-me-now.md.answer: OpenTelemetry was created in early 2019 through the merger of two projects: OpenTracing and OpenCensus.

please-please-otel-me-now.md.question: What is the difference in how Platform Engineers and Site Reliability Engineers use telemetry?
please-please-otel-me-now.md.answer: Platform Engineers focus on building for internal developer teams and care significantly about instrumentation, observability, tracing, and OpenTelemetry. Site Reliability Engineers focus on building for customers and primarily use metrics, logs, dashboards, monitoring, alerting, and various forms of telemetry agents.

underneath-the-cncf-tech-radar.md.question: When was the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) formed and what was its original mission?
underneath-the-cncf-tech-radar.md.answer: The CNCF was formed in 2015 by 22 supporting member companies. Its original mission was to create and drive the adoption of a new computing paradigm optimized for modern distributed systems environments.

underneath-the-cncf-tech-radar.md.question: According to the Gartner survey of CIOs, which categories are seeing increased investments?
underneath-the-cncf-tech-radar.md.answer: The survey identified increased investments in cyber and information security (66%), business intelligence/data analytics (55%), cloud platforms (50%), artificial intelligence (32%), and hyperautomation (24%).

underneath-the-cncf-tech-radar.md.question: What point of confusion did the author raise regarding the CNCF End User Technology Radar for DevSecOps?
underneath-the-cncf-tech-radar.md.answer: The author noted confusion because the 'Trial' range listed 'XRay' as an entry, which linked to XRay (an Idera Company) rather than the JFrog Xray DevSecOps SCA tool, potentially causing ambiguity for readers.

on-twitter.md.question: What did the author do with their primary Twitter output from 2007?
on-twitter.md.answer: The author reset almost all of their primary Twitter output that dated back to 2007.

on-twitter.md.question: Why did the author create archives of their Twitter data?
on-twitter.md.answer: The author created archives to revisit prior analysis and to overcome limitations on web access related to Twitter's rate limits.

on-twitter.md.question: What tool did the author use to analyze their Twitter archives in the most recent attempt?
on-twitter.md.answer: The author used a Python example found on GitHub titled twitter-archive-analysis by dangoldin.

private-podcasting-and-max-headroom.md.question: When did Apple add native support for podcasts to iTunes?
private-podcasting-and-max-headroom.md.answer: Apple added native podcast support to iTunes in 2005.

private-podcasting-and-max-headroom.md.question: What technological advancements allowed for the growth of podcasting?
private-podcasting-and-max-headroom.md.answer: The growth of podcasting was enabled by the increased availability of broadband and the development of publishing syndication, which made it possible to traffic larger files.

private-podcasting-and-max-headroom.md.question: What is the significance of the uStudio Enterprise Podcasting solution mentioned in the post?
private-podcasting-and-max-headroom.md.answer: Introduced in 2018, uStudio provided a secure, private-label podcasting option for enterprises that wanted the ease of audio message syndication while avoiding the pitfalls of wide-open Internet platforms.

soc-it-to-me.md.question: What are the three different definitions of the acronym 'SoC' provided in the text?
soc-it-to-me.md.answer: In the context of the text, SoC stands for 'separation of concerns' in computer science, 'system on a chip' in computer hardware design, and 'security operations center' regarding organizational protection.

soc-it-to-me.md.question: What historical change occurred in mining in 1986 regarding the use of canaries?
soc-it-to-me.md.answer: In 1986, the mining industry ended the practice of using live canaries to detect toxic gases, replacing them with electronic sensors known as 'electronic noses.'

soc-it-to-me.md.question: What is the author's outlook on the future of security operations centers?
soc-it-to-me.md.answer: The author suggests that future security operations centers will increasingly rely on human-machine partnerships, automation, and convergence to effectively correlate events and signal necessary actions.

reading-writing-and-arithmetic.md.question: Why did the author originally decide to pay for YouTube Premium?
reading-writing-and-arithmetic.md.answer: The author initially decided to pay for YouTube Premium primarily to avoid the annoyance of advertising.

reading-writing-and-arithmetic.md.question: What is the focus of Cassie Kozyrkov's YouTube content?
reading-writing-and-arithmetic.md.answer: Cassie Kozyrkov hosts 'Making Friends with Machine Learning,' which consists of over 100 episodes providing over 6 hours of learning about machine learning.

reading-writing-and-arithmetic.md.question: What kind of content does the Rawkode channel provide?
reading-writing-and-arithmetic.md.answer: Rawkode features over 100 hands-on videos covering a variety of open-source cloud-native topics, ranging from Kubernetes fundamentals to DevSpace.

project-liberty-and-dsnp.md.question: What is the primary appeal of the Decentralized Social Networking Protocol (DSNP)?
project-liberty-and-dsnp.md.answer: The appeal of DSNP is that it emphasizes user agency by focusing on user identity, user data rights, and user control, rather than leaving these aspects to a central corporate interest.

project-liberty-and-dsnp.md.question: Who is Frank McCourt in the context of the mentioned social networking projects?
project-liberty-and-dsnp.md.answer: Frank McCourt is associated with Project Liberty and is also noted in the news cycle for his efforts to organize a bid to acquire TikTok.

project-liberty-and-dsnp.md.question: What major challenge do open initiatives like decentralized social networks face?
project-liberty-and-dsnp.md.answer: One of the significant challenges for open initiatives is a lack of funding, as many current social networks rely on advertising and the monetization of clicks and impressions.

its-not-easy-being-greendevsecfinops.md.question: What are the key benefits of applying green coding methodologies to the application lifecycle?
its-not-easy-being-greendevsecfinops.md.answer: The benefits include lowering the overall carbon impact within the SDLC, achieving up to 80% faster performance (especially on mobile or poor network conditions), reducing costs, improving accessibility, and creating a more efficient application without compromising design aesthetics or increasing development time.

its-not-easy-being-greendevsecfinops.md.question: What role do platform engineering teams play in the adoption of green coding?
its-not-easy-being-greendevsecfinops.md.answer: Given that Cloud Sustainability and Platform Engineering appear near each other on Gartner's Hype Cycle for Emerging Tech, the blog suggests it may be up to platform engineering teams to prioritize the consideration of green coding as part of a delightful Developer Experience (DevX).

its-not-easy-being-greendevsecfinops.md.question: How can individuals or organizations get involved in green IT and green coding today?
its-not-easy-being-greendevsecfinops.md.answer: Interested parties can get involved through resources such as Green Software for Practitioners (LFC131), The Green Grid (TGG), the Green Software Foundation (GSF), and the Computational Sustainability Network (CompSustNet). Additionally, developers can start by using tools like co2.js to estimate emissions for software projects.

platform-engineering-blues.md.question: How do Paul Delory and Lori Perry define platform engineering?
platform-engineering-blues.md.answer: Paul Delory defines platform engineering as a response to a perceived need for greater developer productivity through enhanced developer experiences. Lori Perry describes it as the implementation of reusable tools and self-service capabilities with automated infrastructure operations.

platform-engineering-blues.md.question: What is business value engineering in the context of technology?
platform-engineering-blues.md.answer: Business value engineering considers the perceived value provided to a customer or entity through technology, evaluating whether that value is commensurate with the investment and can be proven over the technology's lifecycle, including return on invested capital and total cost of ownership.

platform-engineering-blues.md.question: How has the number of tools on PlatformEngineering.org changed between early 2022 and October 2022?
platform-engineering-blues.md.answer: When PlatformEngineering.org launched on January 30, 2022, the tools list contained four entries. By October 9, 2022, the registry had grown to include twenty-one tools.

thinking-outside-the-cardboard-box.md.question: When was the cardboard box invented?
thinking-outside-the-cardboard-box.md.answer: The cardboard box was born in 1817.

thinking-outside-the-cardboard-box.md.question: What is the significance of the year 2015 for the cardboard box?
thinking-outside-the-cardboard-box.md.answer: In 2015, the package emoji was released in a Unicode update, and Amazon became well-known for memes regarding its packaging practices.

thinking-outside-the-cardboard-box.md.question: How is Amazon using technology to address packaging efficiency?
thinking-outside-the-cardboard-box.md.answer: Amazon has adopted robotics, such as the Kiva robots and the CartonWrap 1000, to improve fulfillment efficiency and address issues like shipping small items in excessively large boxes.

hot-fudge-daily-digest.md.question: What topics are covered in the 'Hot Takes Tuesday' section?
hot-fudge-daily-digest.md.answer: The 'Hot Takes Tuesday' section covers big funding and M&A moves in the tech industry, specifically highlighting developments in cutting-edge language models and cybersecurity startups.

hot-fudge-daily-digest.md.question: Which recent tech event did the author attend?
hot-fudge-daily-digest.md.answer: The author attended the 'Triangle Tech Night: Women Who Innovate' event held at Raleigh Founded.

hot-fudge-daily-digest.md.question: How can readers support Hot Fudge Daily?
hot-fudge-daily-digest.md.answer: Readers can support Hot Fudge Daily by purchasing a $9.99 yearly subscription.

mach-in-2024.md.question: What does the acronym MACH stand for?
mach-in-2024.md.answer: MACH stands for Microservices (based), API (first), Cloud (native and most likely SaaS), and Headless.

mach-in-2024.md.question: What is the purpose of the MACH Alliance?
mach-in-2024.md.answer: According to the MACH Alliance LinkedIn page, the organization advocates for open and best-of-breed enterprise technology ecosystems.

mach-in-2024.md.question: How has the membership of the MACH Alliance changed over time?
mach-in-2024.md.answer: The MACH Alliance has grown from eighteen members in 2020 to over 100 members by 2023, representing a diverse group of ISVs, systems integrators, enablers, and ambassadors.

large-language-marmalade.md.question: What is the author's humorous take on the abbreviation 'LLM'?
large-language-marmalade.md.answer: The author jokingly suggests that 'LLM' stands for 'Large Language Marmalade,' because many businesses try to smear the technology on everything.

large-language-marmalade.md.question: What is the author's stated goal when participating in Help A Reporter Out (HARO)?
large-language-marmalade.md.answer: The author's goal in participating in HARO is to help the reporter, not to provide investment advice or act as an investor.

large-language-marmalade.md.question: What does the author believe regarding the implementation of Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) teams?
large-language-marmalade.md.answer: The author believes that while many teams are being renamed to SRE, it is arguable that the only true SRE work is still being conducted at companies like Google.

get-it-all-while-you-can.md.question: What concept has the author decided to industrialize based on reader feedback regarding data repatriation?
get-it-all-while-you-can.md.answer: The author has decided to industrialize the concept of repatriation from edge to core to cloud as 'continuous commensurate repatriation'.

get-it-all-while-you-can.md.question: What are the primary factors that the author identifies as limiters when moving workloads between the edge, core, and cloud?
get-it-all-while-you-can.md.answer: The author notes that security and finance concerns are the primary limiters to finding an answer for moving workloads between the edge, core, and cloud.

get-it-all-while-you-can.md.question: Besides the traditional '6 Rs' of cloud migration, what four additional 'R's' does the author suggest considering for future data center modalities?
get-it-all-while-you-can.md.answer: The author suggests that four additional 'R's' to consider are Relative, Reallocate, Readied, and Realistic.

notifications-zero.md.question: What is the primary goal of this newsletter digest?
notifications-zero.md.answer: The goal of the digest is to revisit items shared by the author over the course of a day or week and provide additional context regarding why those items are of interest.

notifications-zero.md.question: Why did the author decide to start this email newsletter?
notifications-zero.md.answer: The author started the newsletter because they were told that their Twitter posts were sometimes hard to keep up with and because their personal blog is updated infrequently without an automated way to deliver content to readers' inboxes.

notifications-zero.md.question: What does the author identify as the modern equivalent of an email inbox?
notifications-zero.md.answer: The author identifies notifications—specifically the red circles with numbers on mobile or web browser tools—as the new version of an email inbox.

reflections-on-the-rise-of-modern-scienc.question: What specific history course did the author take thirty years ago?
reflections-on-the-rise-of-modern-scienc.answer: The author took 'HI 322 - THE RISE OF MODERN SCIENCE'.

reflections-on-the-rise-of-modern-scienc.question: What significant technology events occurred in 1993 according to the blog post?
reflections-on-the-rise-of-modern-scienc.answer: Significant events in 1993 included the wider recognition of the World Wide Web, the concept of an 'eternal September', the birth of the Pentium processor, and the initial operational capability of GPS for civilian use.

reflections-on-the-rise-of-modern-scienc.question: What is the purpose of the provided thirty-year listicle?
reflections-on-the-rise-of-modern-scienc.answer: The listicle serves as a highlight reel of loosely related topics from the past 30 years that have shaped the author's career, intended to show events that might compound, converge, or convolute possible futures.

airwaves-vs-airlines.md.question: What is the author's perspective on the future of business travel compared to Cisco Telepresence?
airwaves-vs-airlines.md.answer: The author believes that Cisco Telepresence will increasingly replace the need for face-to-face meetings that require air travel, noting that it will likely become as standard as using a phone within two to three years.

airwaves-vs-airlines.md.question: How does the author view the current state of in-flight WiFi and its pricing?
airwaves-vs-airlines.md.answer: The author notes that road warriors are currently willing to pay for in-flight WiFi, but expects that the current pay-per-use model will eventually disappear or be replaced by packages or membership-based perks.

airwaves-vs-airlines.md.question: Why does the author prefer the Delta Sky Club over working in a coach seat on a plane?
airwaves-vs-airlines.md.answer: The author finds the airline club experience much better for productivity because it offers reliable free wireless and wired internet, whereas working in coach is uncomfortable due to cramped quarters and potential space issues with fellow passengers.

mic-check-123.md.question: How has the technology used for stage snakes changed in recent years?
mic-check-123.md.answer: Engineers now use analog-digital conversion to replace thick, heavy bundles of cables with low-cost Ethernet cables.

mic-check-123.md.question: What is the benefit of using Wi-Fi for live sound mixing?
mic-check-123.md.answer: Using Wi-Fi allows the sound engineer to move freely throughout the room, as the console can be controlled via a tablet from anywhere the Wi-Fi signal reaches.

mic-check-123.md.question: How can a sound engineer communicate with the band while using a mobile tablet to mix sound?
mic-check-123.md.answer: The sound engineer can use a rugged, wireless headset microphone to communicate with the band through the monitors while moving around.

precision-time-protocol-that-you-love.md.question: What protocols and services are discussed in the blog post regarding network time?
precision-time-protocol-that-you-love.md.answer: The post discusses Network Time Protocol (NTP), Precision Time Protocol (PTP), Time Appliance Project (TAP), and Time as a Service (TaaS).

precision-time-protocol-that-you-love.md.question: What is the difference in accuracy between commercial Time as a Service (TaaS) options mentioned?
precision-time-protocol-that-you-love.md.answer: Commercial TaaS options are available in the ~50 µs accuracy range, though some industrial outcomes may require closer to ~1 µs accuracy.

precision-time-protocol-that-you-love.md.question: Why does the Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers (SMPTE) exist in the context of network time?
precision-time-protocol-that-you-love.md.answer: SMPTE exists to handle challenges such as the time synchronization of audio and video streaming over IP networks, with standards like SMPTE ST 2110 driving features for services like AWS Elemental.

quaterly-roundup-edition.md.question: Why did the author deviate from the usual music-inspired layout for this post?
quaterly-roundup-edition.md.answer: The author took a break from the music-inspired layout due to feedback and metrics that intensified their experimental itch.

quaterly-roundup-edition.md.question: What is the author's perspective on the current state of venture capital in developer experience?
quaterly-roundup-edition.md.answer: Despite headlines suggesting venture capital is hibernating, the author notes that this view does not align with the actual data regarding developer experience-related investing.

quaterly-roundup-edition.md.question: What resources does the author suggest to identify future trends in DevOps and platform engineering?
quaterly-roundup-edition.md.answer: The author suggests looking at the DevOps Benchmarking Study 2023, Sequoia's perspectives on AI-powered developer tools, and investments in quantum computing to identify potential future trends.

bufbuild-your-rest.md.question: What is the primary goal of using protocol buffers?
bufbuild-your-rest.md.answer: The goal of protocol buffers is serializing structured data for use in apps for any programming language, providing a path towards extensibility via schemas.

bufbuild-your-rest.md.question: What is the mission of Buf Technologies?
bufbuild-your-rest.md.answer: Buf Technologies' mission is to deprecate REST/JSON and move the industry towards a future of schema-driven development with Protobuf.

bufbuild-your-rest.md.question: How is the buf cli contributing to software development?
bufbuild-your-rest.md.answer: The buf cli is democratizing access to schema-driven development for a wider audience of developers and is helping to improve developer productivity in areas where RESTful APIs are becoming cumbersome.

security-elements-everywhere.md.question: What is the 'SEE' model proposed by the author?
security-elements-everywhere.md.answer: SEE stands for Security Elements Everywhere, a proposed sharing settings system that would allow users to categorize content visibility into five specific tiers: Public, Facebook, Friends Only, Friends of Friends, and Private.

security-elements-everywhere.md.question: Besides granular sharing settings, what visual feature does the author suggest to improve transparency?
security-elements-everywhere.md.answer: The author suggests that Facebook should pictorially show users the specific exposure counts for their information, detailing exactly how many people or which groups will see their posts based on their chosen privacy settings.

security-elements-everywhere.md.question: Why does the author feel that Facebook’s current design philosophy is problematic regarding privacy?
security-elements-everywhere.md.answer: The author believes Facebook's goal is to force opt-out sharing, which contradicts user privacy and results in the platform potentially sharing private information with search engines under the guise of iterative design improvements.

who-am-i-kidding.md.question: What is the primary benefit of the 'Who Am I Kidding Plan' regarding outgoing calls?
who-am-i-kidding.md.answer: The plan automatically sends your calls straight to the other person's voice mail, saving you from waiting for it to ring and preventing you from being a pest.

who-am-i-kidding.md.question: How does the 'Who Am I Kidding Plan' account for the user's social life?
who-am-i-kidding.md.answer: The plan is designed for people who are not popular and rarely receive calls, acknowledging that people often let their calls go straight to voice mail.

who-am-i-kidding.md.question: What happens when the man at the bus stop tries to get the attention of the 'Can you hear me now?' guy?
who-am-i-kidding.md.answer: The Verizon guy ignores him, pretends to take an important call on his phone held upside down, and quickly shuffles away.

ai-feel-you.md.question: What is the price point established by Microsoft for the Microsoft 365 Copilot AI tool?
ai-feel-you.md.answer: Microsoft set a price point of $30 per user per month for the Microsoft 365 Copilot AI tool.

ai-feel-you.md.question: What are some of the 'first' slogans mentioned in the post that have historically captured the zeitgeist of technological focus?
ai-feel-you.md.answer: Examples include Design-first, Web-first, Mobile-first, API-first, Cloud-first, Security-first, Privacy-first, AI-first, and Women-and-Children-first.

ai-feel-you.md.question: What broader educational shift does the post suggest will occur alongside the rise of AI?
ai-feel-you.md.answer: The post suggests that education will expand beyond the traditional focus on reading, writing, and arithmetic to include AI prompting.

whats-golden-path.md.question: How does the Spotify engineering team define a "golden path"?
whats-golden-path.md.answer: Spotify describes a "golden path" as a journey towards conspicuous and continuous improvements in the essential toolchain for a growing software development team.

whats-golden-path.md.question: What is the author's personal definition of a golden path?
whats-golden-path.md.answer: The author defines a golden path as a specific journey towards continuous improvements in an essential toolchain, aimed at increasing software development team velocity, quality, productivity, and joy.

whats-golden-path.md.question: What concern does Kaspar von Grünberg of Humanitec raise regarding golden paths?
whats-golden-path.md.answer: Kaspar von Grünberg highlights the "slippery slope" where golden paths can potentially become "cages" for developers.

gitops-goes-the-world.md.question: What is GitOps?
gitops-goes-the-world.md.answer: GitOps is a practice that combines version control systems favored by developers (Git) with the operations (Ops) of IT practitioners to ensure that everything in the software lifecycle and software versioning currency draws from one true source.

gitops-goes-the-world.md.question: What is the purpose of OpenGitOps?
gitops-goes-the-world.md.answer: OpenGitOps is a CNCF sandbox project that aims to define GitOps in a way that is vendor-neutral.

gitops-goes-the-world.md.question: What is Kyverno?
gitops-goes-the-world.md.answer: Kyverno is a CNCF incubating project that provides a policy engine for Kubernetes governance at scale, allowing users to implement Policy-as-Code (PaC) with hundreds of available policies.

plugins-patterns-and-potpourri.md.question: What is the author's opinion on using Drafts as an alternative to Obsidian?
plugins-patterns-and-potpourri.md.answer: The author realized they are likely not the right market for Drafts and finds that plugins for an existing tool like Obsidian are more compelling than switching to an alternative product.

plugins-patterns-and-potpourri.md.question: What does the author plan to update next month?
plugins-patterns-and-potpourri.md.answer: The author plans to update their annual zettelkasten post.

plugins-patterns-and-potpourri.md.question: Which organization produced the study titled 'Downtowns Rebound: The Data Driven Path to Recovery'?
plugins-patterns-and-potpourri.md.answer: The study was produced by The Center City District (CCD), the Central Philadelphia Development Corporation (CPDC), and the Center City District Foundation (CCDF).

a-matter-of-zero-trust.md.question: What does the author suggest the term 'Zero Trust' means in simpler language?
a-matter-of-zero-trust.md.answer: For the author, 'Zero Trust' is simply a shorter way of saying 'Trust No One'.

a-matter-of-zero-trust.md.question: Why does the author refer to VPNs as a 'vexing productivity neutralizer'?
a-matter-of-zero-trust.md.answer: The author uses this term because of the challenges associated with VPN deployments and the frustration users experience when having to regularly use them for work.

a-matter-of-zero-trust.md.question: According to the post, what company set the early expectation for a user experience that functions without extra software layers like a VPN client?
a-matter-of-zero-trust.md.answer: Google set the expectation bar with their early work on BeyondCorp, which emerged around 2009.

cyber-ground.md.question: What is cyber insurance?
cyber-ground.md.answer: Cyber insurance is a tool used to protect against the growing risks associated with our increasingly data-intensive, online IT infrastructure.

cyber-ground.md.question: How does the author believe cyber insurance will evolve in the future?
cyber-ground.md.answer: The author believes that machine learning and artificial intelligence will eventually provide real-time risk ratings, allowing for a more mature, 'a la carte' approach to cyber insurance policies, similar to modern vehicle telematics.

cyber-ground.md.question: What is the recommended process for voting for the author's SxSW 2024 proposal?
cyber-ground.md.answer: To vote, you must sign up for a free SxSW account, confirm your account via email, and then click the up arrow icon on the proposal page until it turns into a checkbox.

gitops-down-on-it.md.question: What two topics are explored in this blog post?
gitops-down-on-it.md.answer: The blog post focuses on GitOps and the current limits of Generative AI.

gitops-down-on-it.md.question: What was the author's experience when using Google Bard to find learning resources for GitOps?
gitops-down-on-it.md.answer: The author found that while the results initially seemed promising, the actual links were often outdated, broken, or lacked direct sources, leading the author to conclude that further research into Google Bard's results is necessary.

gitops-down-on-it.md.question: What rhetorical devices did the author experiment with to describe GitOps?
gitops-down-on-it.md.answer: The author attempted to use antimetabole and chiasmus to create wordplay describing GitOps, inspired by the military phrase 'slow is smooth and smooth is fast'.

social-telecom.md.question: Why does the author suggest that service providers should monitor social media and customer chatter?
social-telecom.md.answer: The author suggests that monitoring social media chatter allows providers to identify service issues, outages, or anomalies in real-time. By observing patterns in subscriber feedback, providers can better understand network performance, fast-track ticket resolution, and address customer frustrations before they escalate.

social-telecom.md.question: What is the author's view on the future of asymmetric broadband and upload speeds?
social-telecom.md.answer: The author believes that asymmetric broadband will become a thing of the past. As applications shift toward real-time communication, cloud services, and symmetric interactions—such as Skype or video-based applications—the author argues that upload speeds will become just as critical as download speeds.

social-telecom.md.question: How does the author characterize the role of a 'power subscriber' in the modern telecommunications landscape?
social-telecom.md.answer: A 'power subscriber' is an influential user who closely tracks service quality and has a wide audience to share their experiences with. Because they can quickly and efficiently broadcast service issues to a large audience, they force service providers to be more accountable, as their complaints can easily escalate from local discussions to national tech and mainstream press attention.

how-to-buttondown-with-11ty-on-netlify-w.question: What tools are used to create the Fudge Sunday newsletter?
how-to-buttondown-with-11ty-on-netlify-w.answer: The newsletter is written locally using 11ty (Eleventy) with the Eleventy Excellent starter, managed via Visual Studio Code, and hosted using Netlify.

how-to-buttondown-with-11ty-on-netlify-w.question: How does Buttondown automatically create a draft of the newsletter?
how-to-buttondown-with-11ty-on-netlify-w.answer: Buttondown uses the 'RSS to Email' feature; once a post is committed to GitHub and Netlify builds the RSS feed, Buttondown automatically generates a draft newsletter based on that new RSS entry.

how-to-buttondown-with-11ty-on-netlify-w.question: What manual steps remain in the newsletter distribution process?
how-to-buttondown-with-11ty-on-netlify-w.answer: The manual steps include inspecting the generated email draft in Buttondown before sending, copying the content into a LinkedIn newsletter, and crafting two shareable LinkedIn posts.

tomorrow-you-probably-saw-this-already.m.question: What is the relationship between necessity and technology as described in the post?
tomorrow-you-probably-saw-this-already.m.answer: The post asserts that technology is a response to a perceived need. It suggests that when there is a necessity, the future may have already arrived in the past, noting that historical inventions like electric trucks demonstrate that innovations are often born from necessity rather than just modern development.

tomorrow-you-probably-saw-this-already.m.question: Why do some industries continue to use human operators despite the availability of self-service technology?
tomorrow-you-probably-saw-this-already.m.answer: Human operators are still utilized in certain applications, such as elevators, because there are specific service design cases where a human is still better suited to perform tasks that might otherwise be handled by self-service buttons or automation.

tomorrow-you-probably-saw-this-already.m.question: What legal challenge does the rise of AI-generated music present?
tomorrow-you-probably-saw-this-already.m.answer: The rise of AI-produced music, such as the computationally generated death metal mentioned in the post, creates questions regarding copyright and compensation, specifically identifying a potential future conflict over who should be paid for the work: the humans, the monkeys, or the creators of the machines.

zero-tickets-to-paradise.md.question: What is the primary goal of adopting AIOps in relation to developer experience?
zero-tickets-to-paradise.md.answer: The goal is the ruthless removal of developer annoyances in the form of tickets, moving toward a near zero ticket or ticketless developer experience.

zero-tickets-to-paradise.md.question: Why are tickets considered a negative for developer productivity?
zero-tickets-to-paradise.md.answer: Tickets consume time, energy, and productivity cycles. Relying on tickets often creates Ops bottlenecks where developers are forced into a culture of 'waiting as hard as you can' instead of being enabled to complete work or reach outcomes directly.

zero-tickets-to-paradise.md.question: What is required to successfully transition to a ticketless developer experience?
zero-tickets-to-paradise.md.answer: Promoting a ticketless developer experience requires a 'getting things done' (GTD) champion within platform engineering who has executive support.

google-service-dream-weaver.md.question: What performance improvements does Service Weaver claim to offer compared to typical gRPC and protocol buffer microservices?
google-service-dream-weaver.md.answer: Service Weaver claims to improve application latency by 15x and reduce VM costs by 9x when compared to a typical microservices solution built on gRPC and protocol buffers.

google-service-dream-weaver.md.question: Where can developers find official documentation and information regarding Google Service Weaver?
google-service-dream-weaver.md.answer: Developers can find information at the official website (https://serviceweaver.dev), the documentation page (https://serviceweaver.dev/docs.html), and a blog post featuring a 'hello world' guide (https://serviceweaver.dev/blog/quick_intro.html).

google-service-dream-weaver.md.question: According to the post, what is a key factor for the actual growth and adoption of developer tooling?
google-service-dream-weaver.md.answer: According to the post, growth in adoption comes from making a strong business case that includes tooling capable of improving key metrics used to measure developer experience.

own-your-content.md.question: How does the author analyze their traffic patterns?
own-your-content.md.answer: The author analyzes traffic patterns annually, typically approaching October, by reviewing metrics across various platforms like LinkedIn, Twitter, and other experimental services, acknowledging that each platform provides its own unique set of tools and dashboards.

own-your-content.md.question: Why is it becoming more difficult to track referral information?
own-your-content.md.answer: Referral information is increasingly being deprecated because modern browsers are prioritizing user privacy and their own ecosystems, making it necessary to use unique URL tracking to gain insights into content trends.

own-your-content.md.question: What challenge does the author face regarding social media cards and content previews?
own-your-content.md.answer: The author notes that social media card support and cache longevity frequently change, meaning creators have little control over whether their shared content previews correctly or at all on different platforms.

sprawl-walk-run.md.question: What is silo spreadsheet sprawl (SSS)?
sprawl-walk-run.md.answer: Silo spreadsheet sprawl refers to the practice of creating spreadsheets outside of the primary system of record (like an ERP or Salesforce) to add or update data manually, which necessitates further manual entry into other systems.

sprawl-walk-run.md.question: How does writeback help address silo spreadsheet sprawl?
sprawl-walk-run.md.answer: Writeback provides real-time capabilities for end users to contribute, analyze, and visualize data directly within data platforms. This helps organizations avoid the need for external spreadsheets and closes the gap between analysis and action.

sprawl-walk-run.md.question: What are some common business use cases for writeback in the Qlik ecosystem?
sprawl-walk-run.md.answer: Writeback can be used for 'planning' tasks like sales and finance budget planning, as well as 'management' tasks including HR management, audit management, case incident management, and document approval management.

events-and-efficiency.md.question: How is a runtime defined in a C-suite context?
events-and-efficiency.md.answer: A runtime is defined as the amount of time any software application workload takes to run, which is significant because time equates to money.

events-and-efficiency.md.question: Which events can readers attend to connect with the author in 2024?
events-and-efficiency.md.answer: The author is scheduled to attend Red Hat Summit and AnsibleFest (May 6-9 in Denver), Dell Technologies World (May 21-23 in Las Vegas), and Qlik Connect (Jun 3-5 in Orlando).

events-and-efficiency.md.question: What is the primary technical difference between Velox and Comet mentioned in the post?
events-and-efficiency.md.answer: Velox is a C++ library associated with Meta/Facebook, while Comet is a newer Rust library currently under the Apache DataFusion project.

dance-and-technology.md.question: What is the author's plan for the newsletter moving forward?
dance-and-technology.md.answer: The author is rebooting the newsletter for 2019 and plans to release a new installment every Sunday.

dance-and-technology.md.question: Which podcasts and newsletters does the author mention reading or listening to?
dance-and-technology.md.answer: The author listens to the a16z podcast and reads the newsletter by M.G. Siegler. They also mentioned attempting to read Stratechery and the Exponent podcast, though they noted they could not get into them.

dance-and-technology.md.question: What is the primary topic of the 'Dance and Technology' section of the post?
dance-and-technology.md.answer: The section discusses the legal complexities and copyright issues surrounding dance choreography, specifically referencing lawsuits involving dance emotes in video games like Fortnite.

balkanize-rinse-repeat-ad-infinitum.md.question: What is the author's perspective on preserving all internet content for posterity?
balkanize-rinse-repeat-ad-infinitum.md.answer: The author argues that we do not need to save every internet artifact. They suggest that we should let go of things that lose relevance and that the 'wisdom of crowds' on the web does not necessarily equate to things that must be preserved forever.

balkanize-rinse-repeat-ad-infinitum.md.question: How does the author suggest dealing with trolls in online platforms?
balkanize-rinse-repeat-ad-infinitum.md.answer: The author suggests that 'don't feed the trolls' remains a valid strategy. They propose that platforms should implement better moderation and tools, such as the ability to 'squelch' or filter out unwanted content, allowing users to opt-in to seeing only the things they care about.

balkanize-rinse-repeat-ad-infinitum.md.question: What does the author identify as a common historical pattern when new platforms emerge?
